user_query:
We’re standardizing our internal docs around Moodring. Please read input/requests.yaml and input/team_notes.md. Using only the official Moodring reference content for the requested topics (intro, quickstart, patterns, debugging, performance, security), compile a cohesive “Moodring Reference Pack” for engineers.

Deliverables:
1) Write output/moodring_guide.md with:
   - An H1 title taken from requests.yaml.title
   - An executive summary of 80–120 words tailored to onboarding engineers
   - A “## Contents” section listing the included sections in the exact order from requests.yaml.include
   - For each requested topic, create a “## {TitleCase}” heading and include the corresponding Moodring reference content verbatim below it (do not rewrite)
   - Append a “## Team Notes” section that embeds the content of input/team_notes.md verbatim
   - End with a “## Next Steps” section containing 3 concise bullet points
2) Write output/index.json with a JSON object:
   {
     "title": <string>,
     "requested_sections": <array of slugs from requests.yaml.include>,
     "sections": <array of slugs in final order, requested + "team_notes" at the end>,
     "total_sections": <int>,
     "toc_present": true
   }

Conventions:
- Slugs are lowercase: intro, quickstart, patterns, debugging, performance, security, team_notes
- Keep the Moodring reference sections verbatim (no paraphrasing); only the executive summary and Next Steps should be original.
- Do not use any sources other than the Moodring reference content and the provided team notes.

Write only to the specified output/ paths.
